rows.definition: The internal/model interface that selects which arithmetic a single decode block's weight matmuls run through — prep turns a normed activation into the kernel's operand form once, and mul applies a named weight to it — so the f32 and Q8 decode blocks share one block skeleton.
rows.distinction: It is the per-GEMM COMPUTE/arithmetic-dispatch kernel inside the model forward, NOT the coordinator kernel (internal/kernel) that adjudicates and routes tool calls.

rows.definition: The matKernel that runs a decode block fully in f32 — prep is the identity and every matmul routes through parMatRows (the in-order row-parallel GEMV), preserving the bit-exact reduction order.
rows.distinction: It is the f32 arithmetic variant of the model compute kernel, NOT the Q8 (q8Kernel) or int4 (q4Kernel) variant, and not the coordinator kernel.

rows.definition: The matKernel that runs a decode block in Q8_0, quantizing the activation once per prep into a q8Vec and multiplying it against the prebuilt Q8_0 weight via qMatRows.
rows.distinction: It is the Q8_0 compute variant; unlike sessionQ8Kernel it allocates a fresh operand per prep (safe for nested MoE callers), and it is not the f32 (f32Kernel) variant nor the coordinator kernel.

rows.definition: The matKernel that runs a decode block in int4 (Q4): prep is the identity and mul applies the int4-packed weight via the q4 GEMV.
rows.distinction: It is the int4 compute variant of the model forward, distinct from q8Kernel (Q8_0) and the resident Q4_K kernel, and is not the coordinator kernel.

rows.definition: The cacheless Forward matKernel that prefers the exact f32 tensor when present and otherwise reads the quant-on-load Q8_0 resident copy via residentMatRows, keeping ordinary Forward bit-identical.
rows.distinction: It is the host-resident compute endpoint of the host/device split, NOT the GPU-backed backendKernel and not the coordinator kernel.

rows.definition: The matKernel that routes the GLM-MoE-DSA forward's dense GEMMs through a compute.Backend (the GPU pure kernels) instead of host residentMatRows, uploading weight+activation, running be.MatMul, and reading the result back.
rows.distinction: It is the device (GPU) compute endpoint; unlike residentKernel it executes on a compute.Backend, and unlike the coordinator kernel it does matmuls, not tool-call adjudication.

rows.definition: A thin matKernel that dispatches each named matmul per-weight to a host sub-kernel or a device sub-kernel by a predicate, implementing the --n-cpu-moe split (offloaded experts on host RAM, dense projections/router/attention on GPU).
rows.distinction: It introduces no new arithmetic and only decides PLACEMENT between residentKernel and backendKernel; it is not itself a leaf compute kernel nor the coordinator kernel.

rows.definition: The optional device-path interface for GLM-MoE-DSA's learned-indexer score + top-k selection (indexSelect), taken only when the backendKernel's compute.Backend advertises DSAIndexBackend; otherwise the host f64 score/top-k loop runs.
rows.distinction: It is the indexer-SELECTION device seam, distinct from dsaSparseKernel which runs the sparse-attention compute over already-selected keys.

rows.definition: The optional device-path interface for GLM-MoE-DSA's sparse-attention compute (sparseAttend: per-head softmax(scale·q·k)·V over the host-selected keys), taken only when the backend advertises DSASparseBackend.
rows.distinction: It runs the attention math over keys already chosen, distinct from dsaIndexKernel which computes the key SELECTION (index scores + top-k).

rows.definition: The turnbench replay harness's snapshot struct of the coordinator kernel's call-path tallies (submits, vDSO hits, engine calls, denies, transforms) gathered over a replayed trace.
rows.distinction: It tallies the COORDINATOR kernel's dispatch dispositions, not any model compute (matKernel) work; it mirrors kernel.Counters but is the turnbench-side view.

rows.definition: toollint.FromKernel builds tool-lint facts (fast-path tables + preflight schemas) from the process-global kernel registries (vdso.Default and the engine registries) for the registry-only lint view.
rows.distinction: It READS the coordinator kernel's registries to lint tool surfaces; it is not part of the kernel's dispatch path and is unrelated to the model compute kernels.

rows.definition: The id under which internal/modelengine registers internal/model as an abi.EngineDriver, so `--engine inkernel` (or one blank-import) dispatches a tool call to the model fused into the fak process via a real Prefill+Step decode.
rows.distinction: It is the in-process model ENGINE id (a dispatch target), distinct from the coordinator kernel (internal/kernel) that adjudicates calls and from the compute kernels (matKernel) that do the arithmetic.

rows.definition: The agent.Planner backed by the in-kernel model: one Complete runs a greedy Prefill+Step decode over a kernel-owned KV cache using the loaded model + tokenizer.
rows.distinction: It is the planner that drives the in-kernel model decode, distinct from the modelengine EngineDriver wrapper (inkernel) and from proxy/mock planners that call an external upstream.

rows.definition: The request-time error the InKernelPlanner returns when a request's prompt + max-new tokens would exceed device capacity, refusing before any decode allocation.
rows.distinction: It is the PRE-decode capacity refusal (no retry), distinct from InKernelOOMError which is the recovered form of an OOM hit DURING decode.

rows.definition: The agent-level, recovered form of an in-kernel device-allocation OOM during decode, carrying the failed byte count, memory class, and site.
rows.distinction: It is the runtime OOM recovered via idle-pool trim retry, distinct from InKernelCapacityError which refuses oversize requests up front.

rows.definition: The gateway-side function that classifies an error as a recovered in-kernel OOM and extracts its (class, bytes, site) so the metrics layer can count it.
rows.distinction: It is the CLASSIFIER/observer used by gatewayMetrics, not the InKernelOOMError value itself nor the retry mechanism.

rows.definition: The struct reporting idle-pool trim retries attempted after in-kernel device OOMs, bucketed by backend, memory class, and outcome, surfaced through the InKernelOOMRetryReporter interface.
rows.distinction: It counts decode-time OOM RETRIES, distinct from InKernelMemoryPressureTrimStats which counts PROACTIVE idle-pool trims taken before an OOM.

rows.definition: The struct reporting PROACTIVE idle-pool trims the in-kernel planner takes under memory pressure, in bounded-label rows per backend and memory class, surfaced via InKernelMemoryPressureTrimReporter.
rows.distinction: It counts pre-emptive pressure trims, distinct from InKernelOOMRetryStats which counts trims retried AFTER an allocation already OOM'd.

rows.definition: The environment-variable gate that opts the gateway into the in-kernel KV-MMU slot-reclaim bridge (a real SlotFreed -> KV eviction); default-off, where the bridge is an inert no-op.
rows.distinction: It gates the in-kernel KV-MMU reclaim wiring specifically, not the choice of the `inkernel` engine and not the cross-engine KV arena gate FAK_XENGINE_KV.

rows.definition: The environment-variable gate that opts an Arena into becoming the cross-engine zero-copy KV co-residence RegionBackend (the xenginekv package, #448); unset leaves blob as the default.
rows.distinction: It gates the cross-engine KV arena residency, distinct from FAK_INKERNEL_KVMMU which gates the in-kernel KV-MMU slot reclaim.

rows.definition: The string id that keys an inference engine driver in the abi engine registry (e.g. bench.Options.EngineID, the const EngineID="inkernel"); New(engineID) binds a kernel to it.
rows.distinction: It NAMES which registered engine driver to dispatch to, distinct from EngineModel (the model the chosen engine runs) and from EngineRoute (the locality-leading route string).

rows.definition: The coordinator-kernel counter (kernel.Counters.EngineCalls, mirrored into agent/gateway metrics) of tool-call reaps that actually dispatched to the engine rather than being served from the vDSO.
rows.distinction: It counts REAL engine dispatches, the complement of VDSOHits (calls served locally without an engine round-trip).

rows.definition: The model identifier the benchmark engine ran under, recorded in bench.Options.EngineModel and stamped into the report Provenance.
rows.distinction: It records WHICH model the engine executed, distinct from EngineID which names the engine driver itself.

rows.definition: The modelroute.Target method returning the locality-leading route string (e.g. "local:..." / "anthropic:...") that the host stamps into abi.ToolCall.Engine for residency classification.
rows.distinction: It is the per-target ROUTE string (locality + upstream), distinct from EngineID (the registry key) and never carries credential material.

rows.definition: The abi registry function that adds an inference engine driver keyed by id (local/remote/multi); modelengine and others call it from init().
rows.distinction: It registers a general inference EngineDriver, distinct from RegisterReadEngine which registers the specific working-tree-confined read engine.

rows.definition: agent.RegisterReadEngine registers the working-tree-confined read engine under FakReadEngineID, so a cache-missed fak_read dispatches to a real confined disk read.
rows.distinction: It registers ONE specific read engine (fakread), a specialization of the general RegisterEngine.

rows.definition: The const engine id "fakread" that fak_read binds on its abi.ToolCall so the kernel routes the call to the confined read engine.
rows.distinction: It is the id of the read engine specifically, distinct from the general-purpose engine id EngineID (e.g. "inkernel").

rows.definition: The optional abi extension interface (admit -> per-step -> stream -> reclaim) an engine implements when it advertises EngineLifecycleCap, giving the gateway a streaming decode lifecycle.
rows.distinction: It is the optional streaming-lifecycle extension of an EngineDriver, not the base engine itself nor the per-step EngineToken.

rows.definition: One in-flight decode admitted to a LifecycleEngine; its Tokens() channel streams the decode steps.
rows.distinction: It is one admitted decode in flight, distinct from EngineToken (a single streamed step within it).

rows.definition: One streamed decode step from a LifecycleEngine — the generated token id plus optional bytes.
rows.distinction: It is a single decode step, distinct from EngineRequest (the whole in-flight decode that emits a stream of them).

rows.definition: The abi.Capability ("engine.lifecycle.v1") an engine advertises from Caps() when it implements LifecycleEngine, so a consumer can detect lifecycle support.
rows.distinction: It is the advertised capability flag, distinct from the LifecycleEngine interface it gates discovery of.

rows.definition: The gateway's in-process metrics aggregator struct (constructed by newGatewayMetrics) that tallies HTTP, operation, inference, and in-kernel-OOM counters and renders the /metrics Prometheus exposition.
rows.distinction: It is the gateway-scoped metrics state object, distinct from the individual fak_gateway_* metric series it emits and from the coordinator kernel's Counters.

rows.definition: The gateway base URL a client is pointed at — e.g. the dropin child's loopback gateway_url and computeTarget.GatewayURL for a gateway-kind compute target.
rows.distinction: It is the URL value addressing a gateway, distinct from GatewayAddr (the host:port a Planner integrator binds to) and the gateway subsystem itself.

rows.definition: The fak gateway address (host:port, default localhost:8080) the swebench integrator uses to build a Planner against a running gateway.
rows.distinction: It is the swebench host:port used to construct a client, distinct from GatewayURL (the full URL form) and from targetGatewayURL (the compute-target KIND).

rows.definition: The compute-target kind constant ("gateway-url") in cmd/fak/computetarget.go selecting an existing fak/OpenAI-compatible gateway reached over HTTP as the compute backend.
rows.distinction: It is the TARGET-KIND tag choosing a remote gateway endpoint, distinct from GatewayURL (the URL value such a target carries).

rows.definition: The environment variable holding the gateway bearer credential that fak CLI subcommands send when a gateway runs with --require-key.
rows.distinction: It is the credential ENV var (the secret's holder), distinct from defaultGatewayBearerToken() which RESOLVES its value via the secret loader.

rows.definition: The cmd/fak helper that resolves the default bearer credential (from FAK_GATEWAY_KEY via the secret loader) used as the --key default across fak CLI subcommands.
rows.distinction: It is the RESOLVER returning the token value, distinct from FAK_GATEWAY_KEY which is the env var it reads.

rows.definition: The browseraction contract field naming the fak gateway base URL the browser-action agent is pointed at (e.g. http://localhost:8080/v1).
rows.distinction: It is a contract FIELD carrying a gateway URL for the browser-action recipe, not the gateway subsystem itself.

rows.definition: The net/http StatusBadGateway (502) constant the gateway returns when an upstream provider is unreachable, returns an unrecognized tool-call format, or fails cache invalidation.
rows.distinction: It is the stdlib HTTP 502 status signaling an UPSTREAM failure, NOT the fak gateway subsystem; a 502 reports the provider erred, not that fak's gateway is down.

rows.definition: The Prometheus gauge that is 1 when the fak gateway process is scrapeable.
rows.distinction: It reports process liveness only, distinct from fak_gateway_time_to_ready_seconds which measures boot-to-ready latency.

rows.definition: The Prometheus gauge (value 1) carrying static fak gateway build/runtime labels (version, engine, model, vdso).
rows.distinction: It is a static label-carrier gauge, not a dynamic counter like fak_gateway_http_requests_total.

rows.definition: The Prometheus counter of HTTP requests the gateway served, labeled by route, method, and status.
rows.distinction: It counts request COMPLETIONS by status; fak_gateway_http_request_duration_seconds measures their latency and fak_gateway_inflight_requests counts those still executing.

rows.definition: The Prometheus histogram of gateway HTTP request latency, labeled by route, method, and status.
rows.distinction: It measures request LATENCY, distinct from fak_gateway_http_requests_total which only counts requests.

rows.definition: The Prometheus counter of gateway kernel operations labeled by operation, verdict, reason, disposition, and deciding adjudicator (by).
rows.distinction: It counts adjudicated KERNEL operations (the same total the exit summary reports), distinct from fak_gateway_http_requests_total which counts raw HTTP requests.

rows.definition: The Prometheus gauge of HTTP requests currently executing inside the gateway.
rows.distinction: It is a point-in-time in-flight GAUGE, distinct from the cumulative fak_gateway_http_requests_total counter.

rows.definition: The Prometheus gauge of total wall-clock from process start to the gateway becoming ready to serve (0 until ready).
rows.distinction: It is the total boot-to-ready time, distinct from fak_gateway_startup_phase_duration_seconds which breaks that time into named phases.

rows.definition: The Prometheus gauge of each named gateway boot phase's wall-clock cost (flag-parse, policy-load, planner-init, vdso-config, kernel-init, listener-bind, model-load).
rows.distinction: It is the PER-PHASE breakdown, distinct from fak_gateway_time_to_ready_seconds (the total) and fak_gateway_startup_unaccounted_seconds (the residual).

rows.definition: The Prometheus gauge of boot wall-clock not explained by any named startup phase (time_to_ready minus the sum of phase durations); near-zero means startup is fully instrumented.
rows.distinction: It is the RESIDUAL un-instrumented boot time, distinct from the per-phase fak_gateway_startup_phase_duration_seconds.

rows.definition: The Prometheus counter of the gateway's passive rung-decision distribution (rungObs), labeled by rung, kind, and reason.
rows.distinction: It counts KERNEL adjudication-rung decisions, distinct from fak_gateway_operations_total (operations by verdict) and the http_requests counter.

rows.definition: The coordinator-kernel counter of tool calls served from the vDSO fast path without dispatching to the engine.
rows.distinction: It counts locally-served calls, the complement of EngineCalls; it is a raw COUNT, not the VDSOHitRate ratio.

rows.definition: The fraction of kernel submissions served by the vDSO fast path (VDSOHits/Calls), surfaced as bench.Report.VDSOHitRate and the fak_gateway_vdso_hit_ratio / fak_vdso_hit_rate gauges.
rows.distinction: It is the RATIO, distinct from VDSOHits which is the absolute hit count.

rows.definition: The Kernel method that toggles the vDSO fast path on/off (the --vdso ablation, unit 5); off makes every call fall through to adjudication + engine.
rows.distinction: It is the SETTER that flips the path, distinct from VDSOEnabled which reports the current state and from the vdsoOff field it writes.

rows.definition: The Kernel method reporting whether the vDSO fast path is currently active.
rows.distinction: It READS the fast-path state, distinct from SetVDSO which toggles it.

rows.definition: The Kernel field / `--vdso=off` ablation flag that, when set, makes Submit skip the FastPath entirely — the 'off' arm of the A/B benchmark.
rows.distinction: It is the OFF ablation state, the opposite of the vdsoOn benchmark arm; SetVDSO writes it.

rows.definition: The vDSO-enabled benchmark arm — the RunArm vdsoOn=true call labeled "vdso_on" that serves repeats from the fast path.
rows.distinction: It is the ON arm of the bench A/B, the opposite of vdsoOff; both come from the same RunArm.

rows.definition: The turnbench ClassBreakdown count of tier-1 'pure local serve' vDSO hits (e.g. deterministic calculate calls).
rows.distinction: It is the tier-1 pure-compute serve, distinct from VDSODedup (tier-2 duplicate-read cache hit) and VDSOStatic (tier-3 static serve).

rows.definition: The turnbench ClassBreakdown count of tier-2 vDSO hits served from the content cache on a duplicate read (the 'vdso_tier2' source).
rows.distinction: It is the tier-2 duplicate-read cache hit, distinct from VDSOPure (tier-1 pure serve) and VDSOStatic (tier-3 static serve).

rows.definition: The turnbench ClassBreakdown count of tier-3 'static local serve' vDSO hits (e.g. list_all_airports).
rows.distinction: It is the tier-3 static serve, distinct from VDSOPure (tier-1) and VDSODedup (tier-2 dedup).
